summary: The jump from 621 units abated in FY22 to over 5,000 by FY24-FY25 reflects a citywide law (Local Law 66) that took effect December 1, 2021 and lowered the legal threshold defining lead-based paint from 1.0 to 0.5 mg/cm2, which meant far more NYCHA apartments suddenly required remediation. NYCHA's own reporting says it ramped up abatement work specifically to comply with this lower threshold.

sources.label: NYC Dept. of Housing Preservation and Development, Lead-Based Paint Threshold FAQ
